sections.title: What is a proforma invoice?
sections.body: A proforma invoice is a preliminary bill of sale sent before goods or services are delivered. It states the items, prices, taxes and total so the buyer can arrange payment, a deposit, financing or customs clearance. It is not a final tax invoice and does not record a sale in your books, it is a commitment to the price and terms of an upcoming transaction.

sections.title: Proforma, deposit, final invoice
sections.body: Paper & Pen keeps the proforma connected to the final invoice so a deposit and a balance reconcile cleanly.
items: Create a proforma from a quote or from scratch
Set currency, tax and the advance or full amount
Send by WhatsApp or email with a pay-online link
Collect a deposit and record it against the order
Convert the proforma to a final tax invoice when you deliver

sections.title: Common uses for a proforma invoice
sections.body: Proforma invoices are widely used in international trade and project work: to request a deposit before production, to support a customs declaration for a shipment, to give a buyer a document for their financing or purchase approval, and to confirm price and terms before a final tax invoice is raised on delivery.

faqs.q: Is a proforma invoice the same as a final invoice?
faqs.a: No. A proforma is a preliminary, advance bill that confirms price and terms before delivery. The final tax invoice is the legal record of the sale and is what posts to your accounts.

faqs.q: Is the proforma invoice maker free?
faqs.a: Yes. Creating, sending and tracking proforma invoices is free forever on Paper & Pen.

faqs.q: Can I collect a deposit on a proforma?
faqs.a: Yes. Add a pay-online link so the client can settle a deposit or the full advance, and the payment records against the order.

faqs.q: How do I convert a proforma to a tax invoice?
faqs.a: One click converts the proforma into a final, numbered tax invoice, carrying over every line, tax and any deposit already paid.

faqs.q: Can I use a proforma for customs?
faqs.a: Yes. Proforma invoices are commonly used for customs declarations and shipment documentation. Set the currency and item details the importing country requires.
